channel.title: Aesthetics on Nils Myszkowski, PhD
channel.description: Recent content in Aesthetics on Nils Myszkowski, PhD

item.title: Can We “Read” Art...At All? A Psychometric Perspective on the Possibility of Measuring Artwork Attributes
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we examine the claim that we can “read” artworks and evaluate what is psychometrically required for such measurement claims to be valid and interpretable. Abstract This article offers a psychometric commentary on the possibility of measuring artwork attributes. It focuses on the conceptual and measurement assumptions needed to support inferences about what can be extracted from artworks through assessment. The discussion highlights construct-definition issues, score-interpretation limits, and the importance of formal measurement models when evaluating claims about art-based judgments.

item.title: Using Visual Aesthetic Sensitivity Measures in Museum Studies
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we examine the use of visual aesthetic sensitivity measures in museum contexts and outline psychometric issues that matter when selecting and interpreting these instruments. Abstract Visual aesthetic sensitivity has long been assessed with tests intended to capture individual differences in judgments of artistic quality. This article discusses how such measures can be used in museum studies and what kinds of validity evidence are needed for defensible interpretation.

item.title: The First Glance Is the Weakest: “Tasteful” Individuals Value Aesthetics and Make Stronger Aesthetic Judgments After Longer Visual Inspection
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we examine the first glance is the weakest. Abstract This paper examines the focal construct and reports empirical and/or psychometric evidence relevant to its interpretation. The findings contribute to the literature by clarifying measurement, theoretical implications, and practical applications in context. How to access the paper You can access the full paper here.

item.title: Measuring “Good Taste” with the Visual Aesthetic Sensitivity Test-Revised (VAST-R)
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we assess how well the VAST-R measures individual differences in visual aesthetic sensitivity. Abstract The Visual Aesthetic Sensitivity Test-Revised (VAST-R) is used to assess differences in aesthetic judgment quality. This article evaluates its psychometric behavior and discusses implications for interpreting “good taste” as an individual-differences construct. The paper addresses reliability, validity, and practical use in research contexts where visual aesthetic sensitivity is a focal variable.

item.title: Individual Differences in Aesthetic Ability: The Case for an Aesthetic Quotient
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we examine individual differences in aesthetic ability. Abstract This paper examines the focal construct and reports empirical and/or psychometric evidence relevant to its interpretation. The findings contribute to the literature by clarifying measurement, theoretical implications, and practical applications in context. How to access the paper You can access the full paper here.

item.title: Is Visual Aesthetic Sensitivity Independent from Intelligence, Personality, and Creativity?
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we examine is visual aesthetic sensitivity independent from intelligence, personality, and creativity?. Abstract This paper examines the focal construct and reports empirical and/or psychometric evidence relevant to its interpretation. The findings contribute to the literature by clarifying measurement, theoretical implications, and practical applications in context. How to access the paper You can access the full paper here.

item.title: How Personality Traits Predict Design-Driven Consumer Choices
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we examine how personality traits predict design-driven consumer choices. Abstract This paper examines the focal construct and reports empirical and/or psychometric evidence relevant to its interpretation. The findings contribute to the literature by clarifying measurement, theoretical implications, and practical applications in context. How to access the paper You can access the full paper here.
